violations.text: The pre-flush spray hose at the dishwasher is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. PIC stated he mailed his application yesterday. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Molluscan Shellfish tags did not have all of the required information. Dates of last use were lacking. Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Food contact surfaces of the crushed ice machine were observed with an accumulation of soil residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026
The kitchen cork board was not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2026
One wood cutting board was not in good repair, with cracks in it, impacting its ability to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

The pre-flush spray hose at the dishwasher is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026
One light beneath the hood was nonfunctional during inspection. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. Manager arrived during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

An employee was observed eating in the kitchen food prep area. An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Molluscan Shellfish tags did not have all of the required information. Dates of last use were lacking. Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Numerous open containers of food were observed in prep units and chest freezers. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or packaging. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, knives in the clean storage container) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Food contact surfaces of the crushed ice machine were observed with an accumulation of soil residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Working containers of ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Utensils in containers in the kitchen are displayed with the food contact surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026
Small pizza pans were not in good repair, with edges bent impacting their ability to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026
A wood cutting board was not in good repair, with cracks, impacting its ability to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:grillarea beneath the grillutensil storage binssalad prep unitThe beer tap cooler has an accumulation of liquid.Chest freezers have an accumulation of frost and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

The pre-flush spray hose at the dishwasher sink is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026
One light beneath the hood was nonfunctional during inspeciton. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the kitchen and basement dry storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings along the ledges in the basement stairwells, and a gnawed wall along the stairwell. Guardian PCS last date of service 11/19/2024. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

A knife observed stored on an unclean surface (between prep unit lids) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024
Single service items are not being stored properly, unwrapped in the basement storage area. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024
Two lightbulbs are missing from the light sockets beneath the hood. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2024

The ledges on the basement stairwells, and ceiling in the basement especially near the ice machine area have an accumulation of soil residue, cobwebs, dust, and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment (mixer) or items were observed in the kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2024

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2024

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Spanish Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Open employees' beverage containers were observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (salad mix) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink at the bar. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
The hand-washing sink at the bar was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
An open container of soup adulterated with frost observed in the chest freezer. Disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
Shellstock tags did not have all of the required information. Dates of last use were lacking. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Open containers of food observed in freezers. Open containers of food observed stacked on top of one another with the top containers in the food below. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or wrapping. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Microgreens labeled Keep Refrigerated observed held at 58 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
Working containers (spray bottles) used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
ROP tuna observed with intact packaging in the walk in cooler.Disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings on the ledge along the basement stairs, and gnawed material. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies.Guardian PCS last date of service 10/30/2024 per PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
An employee observed using an immersion blender in containers of food on the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

A potato chopper was observed in the basement on a fiberboard wall, in an area unapproved for food preparation.The potato chopper must be removed from the basement.No food production is allowed in the basement. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Knife observed stored on an unclean surface (between lids on prep unit) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the kitchen and basement storage areas. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the prep units have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris.Chest freezer has an accumulation of frost and debris.Shelves in the kitchen are covered with plastic that is shredded with pieces missing. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
Water observed leaking from ceiling pipe in the basement into a container. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/08/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen, outside near the walk in cooler, and basement storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2024

The area outside near the walk in cooler has an accumulation of litter, leaf litter, and debris.The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2024
Light bulbs missing in one light beneath the hood, and in the basement storage area. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2024
